senses.definition: A misspelling or very rare variant related to mineral names beginning with "anorth-", not used in normal modern English.
examples: The term "anorth" does not appear in standard geology textbooks.
He assumed "anorth" was a typo for a longer mineral name.

senses.definition: Relating to or having a crystal structure that is not at right angles, especially in reference to triclinic crystals.
examples: The mineral shows anorthic symmetry under microscopic analysis.
Anorthic structures can be difficult for students to visualize.
These anorthic crystals contrast with the more common orthorhombic forms.

senses.definition: A pale feldspar mineral that is the calcium-rich end member of the plagioclase series, common in some igneous rocks.
examples: The rock sample contained large crystals of anorthite near the margins.
Anorthite is often found in dark volcanic rocks from the ocean floor.
The lab report identified the white mineral as anorthite using X-ray diffraction.

senses.definition: Plural of anorthite, referring to more than one crystal or occurrence of this feldspar mineral.
examples: The thin section showed several anorthites scattered through the matrix.
Rare anorthites were documented at the new volcanic site.
These anorthites appear more altered than the surrounding plagioclase.

senses.definition: Containing much anorthite or having a composition rich in the anorthite component of plagioclase feldspar.
examples: The lava flow consists mainly of anorthitic plagioclase.
Anorthitic rocks are common in some lunar highlands samples.
She described the feldspar as strongly anorthitic in composition.

senses.definition: An igneous rock made almost entirely of plagioclase feldspar, especially of anorthitic composition.
examples: The massif is dominated by nearly pure anorthitite layers.
Lunar samples include fragments of coarse-grained anorthitite.
Anorthitite forms when plagioclase accumulates during crystal settling.

senses.definition: A feldspar mineral from the alkali feldspar group, rich in sodium and potassium and often found in volcanic rocks.
examples: Anorthoclase commonly appears in certain volcanic lavas.
The sanidine and anorthoclase in this rock show similar optical properties.
Anorthoclase crystals may show fine exsolution lamellae.

senses.definition: Describing a way of writing or spelling that does not match normal or standard orthographic rules.
examples: The manuscript is full of anorthographic spellings.
Anorthographic forms can show how pronunciation has changed.
The editor corrected many anorthographic variants in the text.

senses.definition: Relating to nonstandard or irregular spelling or writing forms.
examples: He examined the anorthographical features of the dialect.
Anorthographical spelling can be seen in early letters.
These anorthographical patterns may confuse learners.

senses.definition: In a way that does not follow the usual rules of spelling or writing.
examples: Many words are written anorthographically in the diary.
The term is often used anorthographically in informal texts.
These names were recorded anorthographically by the clerk.

senses.definition: The use or study of spelling that does not follow normal or accepted orthographic rules.
examples: The article focuses on anorthography in online communication.
Anorthography can reveal how sounds are perceived by speakers.
He collected examples of anorthography from social media posts.

senses.definition: A type of basic igneous rock rich in plagioclase feldspar, especially anorthite, sometimes used as a variant term for anorthosite.
examples: The intrusion consists mainly of anorthophyre with minor pyroxene.
Anorthophyre bodies are mapped along the craton margin.
Thin sections of the anorthophyre show abundant plagioclase laths.

senses.definition: A visual disorder in which straight lines appear curved or objects look distorted.
examples: Patients with anorthopia may see walls as bending inward.
Anorthopia can result from damage to parts of the visual cortex.
The neurologist noted signs of anorthopia during the visual test.

senses.definition: An optical device with rotating disks that turns a stretched, distorted picture into a normal-looking image.
examples: The museum demonstrated an old anorthoscope to visitors.
An anorthoscope uses rotating slits to correct an anamorphic picture.
Students built a simple anorthoscope for their physics project.

senses.definition: A rare or older term used for certain feldspar minerals related to anorthite.
examples: The label "anorthose" appears on some nineteenth-century mineral samples.
Modern texts rarely use the term anorthose.
Anorthose was once applied to plagioclase varieties in French literature.

senses.definition: A light-colored igneous rock made mostly of plagioclase feldspar, often forming large bodies in the Earth's crust and the Moon's crust.
examples: The mountain range is built largely of anorthosite.
Lunar anorthosite samples come from the Moon's highlands.
Anorthosite bodies are important for understanding crustal evolution.

senses.definition: Plural of anorthosite, referring to more than one body or occurrence of this feldspar-rich rock.
examples: Similar anorthosites occur on several continents.
These anorthosites show evidence of large-scale crystal settling.
The study compares Archean anorthosites from different cratons.

senses.definition: Made of or similar to anorthosite, or rich in plagioclase feldspar like anorthosite.
examples: The anorthositic crust of the Moon is rich in plagioclase.
Anorthositic layers alternate with more mafic rocks in the complex.
Anorthositic rocks can be difficult to date precisely.

senses.definition: A short medical tube used to look inside the anus and lower rectum.
examples: The doctor used an anoscope to examine the rectal area.
An anoscope allows direct viewing of internal hemorrhoids.
The anoscope was gently inserted for a brief inspection.

senses.definition: A medical procedure in which a doctor uses an anoscope to look inside the anus and lower rectum.
examples: Anoscopy can help diagnose causes of rectal bleeding.
The clinic offers anoscopy as an outpatient procedure.
Anoscopy is often done without general anesthesia.

senses.definition: A condition in which a person is not aware of their own illness or disability, especially after brain damage.
examples: Due to anosia, the patient denied any weakness in his arm.
Anosia can complicate rehabilitation after a stroke.
The therapist noted signs of anosia during the assessment.

senses.definition: Having no sense of smell.
examples: Anosmatic patients may lose interest in food.
He has been anosmatic since a head injury.
Some insects are rendered anosmatic by certain chemicals.

senses.definition: The complete loss of the sense of smell.
examples: After the infection, she developed anosmia and could not smell anything.
Anosmia can affect appetite and enjoyment of food.
Covid-19 brought much public attention to anosmia as a symptom.

senses.definition: Plural of anosmia, referring to different cases or types of loss of smell.
examples: Post-infectious anosmias are now more widely recognized.
Various anosmias may have different underlying causes.
Clinicians distinguish between congenital and acquired anosmias.

senses.definition: Not able to smell anything.
examples: He has been anosmic since childhood.
Anosmic patients may not notice gas leaks.
Once anosmic, she had to rely on others to check if food was fresh.

senses.definition: A condition in which a person with a serious illness is not aware of it or denies it, often after brain damage.
examples: Anosognosia is common after strokes affecting the right hemisphere.
Because of anosognosia, he insisted he could walk unaided.
Treating anosognosia requires patience and careful explanation.

senses.definition: Loss or serious disturbance of the ability to speak due to brain damage.
examples: Anosphrasia can result from injury to key language areas.
Patients with anosphrasia may understand speech but find it hard to respond.
Severe anosphrasia often requires long-term speech therapy.

senses.definition: Complete loss of the sense of smell.
examples: Anosphresia may follow infections or head injuries.
The smell test confirmed anosphresia in the patient.
Long-term anosphresia can affect quality of life.

senses.definition: Relating to both the anus and the spinal cord.
examples: The syndrome includes anospinal malformations.
Anospinal defects are described in some congenital conditions.
Surgeons considered the anospinal relationship during the procedure.

senses.definition: A condition in which bone fails to form or develop normally.
examples: Radiographs showed signs of anostosis in the affected limb.
Anostosis can lead to deformities and weakness.
The rare disorder involves partial anostosis of the skull.

senses.definition: An order of small freshwater crustaceans that includes fairy shrimp.
examples: Anostraca live in temporary ponds that dry up seasonally.
The biologist specializes in Anostraca and other branchiopods.
Several Anostraca species are found in desert pools.

senses.definition: A rare mineral name, now little used, for a variety related to feldspar-group minerals.
examples: Only a few specimens labelled anoterite exist in collections.
The name anoterite is largely historical.
Older literature occasionally refers to anoterite in granite.

senses.definition: One more person or thing of the same type.
examples: Would you like another cup of tea?
She bought another dress for the party.
I need another minute to finish this.

senses.definition: One more person or thing, when it is clear what type you mean.
examples: If this pen does not work, try another.
Some students passed, but another failed.
I do not like this seat; I will find another.

senses.definition: A nonstandard or playful form meaning another attempt to guess.
examples: You only get one anotherguess at the answer.
He asked for anotherguess before the solution was revealed.
The game allows each player a single anotherguess.

senses.definition: A very rare or dialect word meaning another small amount, piece, or kind of something.
examples: He asked for anotherkins of bread from the basket.
The story adds anotherkins of detail in each retelling.
She poured anotherkins into his cup.

senses.definition: A birth defect where the external ear is completely missing.
examples: Anotia may occur on one or both sides of the head.
Surgery can sometimes reconstruct the ear in cases of anotia.
The child was born with anotia of the right ear.

senses.definition: A condition in which one or both eyes turn upward abnormally.
examples: The child was diagnosed with anotropia by the eye specialist.
Surgery may correct severe anotropia.
Anotropia can cause double vision if left untreated.

senses.definition: A variant spelling of annatto, a natural red-orange dye made from the seeds of the achiote tree, used in food and cosmetics.
examples: The cheese is colored with anotta extract.
Traditional recipes use anotta as a natural coloring.
Anotta gives the butter its rich yellow shade.

senses.definition: Another old or variant form of annatto, a natural reddish dye from achiote seeds used mainly in foods.
examples: Older cookbooks refer to anotto for coloring cheese.
The factory imports anotto in large quantities.
Anotto has long been used as a natural food dye.

senses.definition: Having no ear or lacking the normal external ear.
examples: Anotus individuals may require hearing assessments.
The condition was described as anotus on one side.
Anotus embryos were observed in the study.

senses.definition: An old or dialect spelling of "enough".
examples: He had anough to eat and drink for the night.
The poet wrote "anough" instead of "enough" in the original text.
They said they were anough tired to sleep anywhere.

senses.definition: A rare regional term, possibly referring to a type of small animal or spirit in folklore, not used in standard English.
examples: The legend tells of an anounou living in the forest.
Children were warned that the anounou would come at night.
He claimed to see an anounou near the village.

senses.definition: Having no tail, especially referring to animals.
examples: The anour frog species lack visible tails as adults.
Anour mammals are uncommon in nature.
The specimen is almost anour because its tail is so short.

senses.definition: A taxonomic group name used for certain tailless bats, especially in scientific classification.
examples: Several species in Anoura feed on nectar.
Anoura is found in tropical regions of the Americas.
Researchers trapped an Anoura specimen near the cave.

senses.definition: French-influenced or rare form meaning having no tail.
examples: The anoure amphibians of this region are well studied.
Anoure forms are adapted to a burrowing lifestyle.
The text refers to anoure fish in underground streams.

senses.definition: Without a tail, especially used of animals.
examples: The anourous lizard species rely on camouflage.
Some anourous fish live in dark cave systems.
Anourous forms appear in several unrelated groups.

senses.definition: A genus of tropical seabirds known as noddies.
examples: Anous includes several species of noddies.
Birdwatchers observed a flock of Anous over the reef.
Conservationists study Anous populations on remote islands.

senses.definition: Short for analysis of variance, a statistical method used to compare the averages of three or more groups.
examples: We used ANOVA to test for differences between the groups.
The results of the ANOVA were highly significant.
A one-way ANOVA was performed on the data set.

senses.definition: Relating to both the anus and the urinary bladder.
examples: The surgeon repaired an anovesical fistula.
Anovesical problems can cause serious infection.
Anovesical connections were identified on imaging.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that prevents ovulation, often used in birth control.
examples: She takes an anovulant as part of her contraceptive plan.
The doctor prescribed an anovulant to regulate her cycle.
Long-term use of an anovulant should be monitored by a physician.

senses.definition: Preventing ovulation.
examples: The anovulant effect of the drug reduces fertility.
Anovulant agents are commonly used in contraception.
This treatment has both anovulant and other hormonal actions.
